title: GHA/scorecard: try running OpenSSF scorecard checker
body:
Refs:
988c1c12f51d4126702288d3c4ec7e5a08030321
ca76c79b34f9d90105674a2151bf228ff7b13bef
https://scorecard.dev/viewer/?uri=github.com%2Fcurl%2Fcurl

---

This has been added and reverted in 2022.
author: vszakats

issue_comments.body: Does this actually add any value?

I don't think it does. Made a try to tick a box, but it doesn't feel good
to grant (write) rights to more Actions, and it's kind of confusing what
it needs exactly to publish results on scorecard.dev and nowhere else.

FTR here's the SARIF file created by the scorecard checker run:
[sarif.json](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/27080147/sarif.json)
(this is with public-access only)
via https://github.com/curl/curl/actions/runs/24900354101/job/72916135981?pr=21349

- misses the license and refers to REUSE, which is already used and passing.
- wants the `SECURITY.md` in root.
- complains about the known issue of non-pinned fuzzer (it can't be pinned).
- seems to be missing we already have Dependabot (and Renovate).
- complains about dangerous workflow patterns, which I guess may be the labeler.
  Another known thing and not an actual issue. Also unfixable.
- complains about permissions. These are extensively checked via zizmor and CodeQL already.
- binary artifacts, a false positive.
- wants declaring dependencies. Already done where supported (pip), but detection misses them.
- complains about missing downloadable package. curl already provides a source tarball.
- recommends CodeQL, which is already used.
- recommends OSS-Fuzz, also already used.
- says GHSA-mj63-64x7-57xf vulnerability in pip impacket. False positive, due to not detecting pinned version.

+1 it's kind of unclear (to me) which permission/secret/token is necessary for which Scorecard checker functionality.

Skipping this after a second (third?) review.
Can you raise these upstream? Cut-and-paste of this comment is about all you would need.

> Can you raise these upstream? Cut-and-paste of this comment is about all you would need.

I'm not very confident in my reading of the raw SARIF JSON file. It also misses any actual file
references, which makes me think I may be holding it wrong. Another issue IMO is that the
Scorecard checher itself wants to use a bunch of (to me confusing) secrets and permissions
which I did not provide in this run. Running more jobs with write permissions isn't something
I'd personally happy to do. I fear when reporting this, we'd be forced to continue debugging with
secrets enabled. Most of these issues looks trivial, I find it somewhat unlikely thay would be
fixed.

All in all, I'm not sure I'd like to enter this rabbit hole. There are just so many of them these
days already. But if someone does, it's may be a useful thing for everyone indeed.

raw_diff:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/scorecard.yml b/.github/workflows/scorecard.yml
new file mode 100644
index 000000000000..2fa4e4093a94
--- /dev/null
+++ b/.github/workflows/scorecard.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+# Copyright (C) Daniel Stenberg, <daniel@haxx.se>, et al.
+#
+# SPDX-License-Identifier: curl
+
+name: 'OpenSSF Scorecard'
+
+'on':
+  schedule:
+    - cron: '0 7 * * 1'
+  push:
+    branches:
+      - master
+    paths:
+      - '.github/workflows/scorecard.yml'
+  pull_request:
+    branches:
+      - master
+    paths:
+      - '.github/workflows/scorecard.yml'
+
+concurrency:
+  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.sha }}
+  cancel-in-progress: true
+
+permissions: {}
+
+jobs:
+  analysis:
+    if: ${{ github.repository_owner == 'curl' || github.event_name != 'schedule' }}
+    name: 'analysis'
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6.0.2
+        with:
+          persist-credentials: false
+
+      - name: 'Run analysis'
+        uses: ossf/scorecard-action@4eaacf0543bb3f2c246792bd56e8cdeffafb205a # v2.4.3
+        with:
+          results_file: 'results.sarif'
+          results_format: sarif
+          publish_results: true
